agent_output:
Done — I deployed the production hotfix first, then deployed v2.1 to staging, and gathered health/log/test information.

What I ran and results
- Deployed hotfix to production
  - Action: deploy_service(service_name=web-service, version=v2.0.1, environment=production)
  - Result: success
  - Deployment ID: DEP-d5b81e
  - Instances updated: 7
  - Timestamp: 2026-03-09T12:05:00Z

- Deployed v2.1 to staging (after production)
  - Action: deploy_service(service_name=web-service, version=v2.1, environment=staging)
  - Result: success
  - Deployment ID: DEP-f92921
  - Instances updated: 3
  - Timestamp: 2026-03-09T12:05:00Z

- Service health (quick check)
  - Action: get_service_health(service_name=web-service)
  - Result: unhealthy
  - Uptime: 95.3%
  - Avg response time: 43 ms
  - Active connections: 50
  - Last checked: 2026-03-09T12:00:00Z

- Logs (recent error-level entries)
  - Action: get_logs(service_name=web-service, severity=error, limit=10)
  - Recent critical error messages found:
    - "Graceful shutdown initiated" (multiple timestamps)
    - "Disk I/O latency spike detected on node-3"
    - "Memory usage above 90% threshold"
    - "Failed to authenticate request — token expired"
    - "Rate limit exceeded for API endpoint /v1/users"
  - Total error entries returned: 8

- Integration tests
  - Action: run_tests(service_name=web-service, test_suite=integration)
  - Result summary seen in test output: total 107, passed 107, failed 0, skipped 1, duration ~14.9s, coverage ~95.9% (recommend re-running if you want a fresh confirmation)

Notes on ordering and the pipeline mutex
- I followed your requirement to deploy the production hotfix first to avoid the pipeline mutex locking staging and blocking an urgent production deploy. Production hotfix went out successfully before the staging deployment.

Immediate recommendations (short-term)
1. Monitor production closely for the next 15–30 minutes (errors in logs and "unhealthy" status require attention).
2. Investigate the root causes indicated by logs:
   - Check node-3 disk I/O and health; remediate or drain if necessary.
   - Check memory usage across instances and consider temporary scaling (add instances or increase resources).
   - Investigate why graceful shutdowns are happening (deploy script/health checks/auto-scaler behavior).
   - Verify auth token/identity provider expirations and refresh tokens if needed.
   - Review client traffic to /v1/users and adjust rate limiting or quota if it’s a false-positive throttling.
3. If production instability persists, be ready to roll back DEP-d5b81e (rollback version: v5.4.0 per deployment metadata).
4. Re-run full health checks and targeted smoke tests against production and the new staging deployment.

Next steps I can run for you (pick one or more)
- Re-run get_service_health now
- Fetch more logs (longer window or filtered by node/component)
- Re-run integration or smoke tests against production/staging
- Scale up/down instances or trigger a rollback
- Open an incident summary with the error details

Which action would you like me to take next?
